title: Git Hub Repo - wincc SRTM_Monitor_EC_01
content:
ITEM:
- Git hub repo to store a simple project than be used at EMF.


DATE:
Thursday-March-05-2026

https://github.com/echeu/wincc-srtm

title: Pelagic-Vocal-mk1 — Offline Pipeline Status
preview: openWakeWord → Whisper STT → Piper TTS chain functional. CPU latency ~1.2s round-trip. LLM integration is next phase.
content:
STACK: openWakeWord → Whisper (base) → Piper TTS
All components running fully offline on CPU.

LATENCY BENCHMARKS:
  Wake word detection  ~40ms
  STT transcription    ~800ms
  TTS synthesis        ~300ms
  Round-trip total     ~1.2s

NEXT MILESTONES:
  [x] Offline STT+TTS baseline
  [ ] Local LLM backend (Ollama / llama.cpp)
  [ ] Electron/HTML5 frontend with visual state
  [ ] Persistent conversation context
  [ ] Hardware wake-word button (RPi GPIO)

title: KS Comparison Scripts — v6 Findings
preview: Data vintage differences (2023 CSV vs Run 3 extraction) are the dominant driver of KS failures, not cut mismatches.
content:
CONCLUSION: The primary cause of KS test failures in the MSVtx cutflow comparison is data vintage mismatch, not incorrect cut logic.

Diptiman's 2023 CSVs were generated with a different sample and slightly different preselection configuration than the current Run 3 extraction via Snapshot after df11.

AFFECTED MASS POINTS:
  mH125_mS55  (DSID 537839) — investigated ✓
  mH125_mS35  — pending
  mH125_mS16  — pending
  mH125_mS5   — pending

FILES: ~/atlas_nn/0_mk2/0_comparative_hists/
SCRIPT: ks_compare_v6.py

title: INFO 539 — PA2 SMS Spam Classifier
preview: CountVectorizer(1,2) + LogisticRegression. Key insight: _augment_text meta-tokens + class_weight={0:1,1:10} → F1=0.944.
content:
FINAL CONFIG:
  Vectorizer:    CountVectorizer(ngram_range=(1,2))
  Classifier:    LogisticRegression(C=0.3)
  Class weight:  {0: 1, 1: 10}  ← critical for recall

PERFORMANCE:
  F1 (spam):   0.944
  Accuracy:    0.986

KEY INSIGHT: The _augment_text() meta-token injection
(e.g. __HAS_URL__, __HAS_PHONE__, __ALL_CAPS__) was the
single biggest performance lever. Raw n-grams alone top
out around F1=0.88; augmented features break 0.94.

LESSON: Domain-specific feature engineering still beats
raw vectorization for short-text classification tasks.

title: TRON Ares RAM Monitor — Heat Map Variants
preview: Evaluating four heat map display modes: A=radial thermal field, B=memory block grid, C=heat strip history, D=hybrid.
content:
HEAT MAP VARIANTS UNDER EVALUATION:

  A — Radial Thermal Field
      Circular gradient from center; intensity = RAM %
      Looks great but less information-dense

  B — Memory Block Grid
      NxN grid of cells, each cell = memory page bucket
      Most data-rich; closest to actual memory map

  C — Heat Strip History
      Horizontal time-series of usage intensity bars
      Good for detecting usage spikes over time

  D — Hybrid
      Block grid (B) overlaid on radial glow (A)
      Visually the strongest; performance cost?

CURRENT STATUS: Prototyping B and D in parallel.
MVC pattern confirmed working. PyQt6 border thickness
and hover button state resolved in last session.

title: Fence Method — Cubic Root Bounds
preview: r₁=−2a parameterization → fence bounds → absorption substitution u=t−p/(3t). Root-level approaches are circular; absorption transforms the polynomial itself.
content:
CORE INSIGHT: Attempting to bound roots directly is circular
(you need the roots to find the bounds). The fence method 
sidesteps this by parameterizing with r₁ = −2a, then using 
the absorption substitution u = t − p/(3t) to transform 
the polynomial rather than its roots.

THEOREM 1 (Fence Bound, real roots):
  Given t³ + pt + q = 0 with 3 real roots,
  the fence r₁ = −2√(−p/3) gives:
    r₁ ≤ x_min  and  x_max ≤ −r₁/2

THEOREM 2 (Sandwich Bound, complex roots):
  For the complex pair α ± βi:
    |α| ≤ (|q|/|a|)^(1/2)

GEOMETRIC CONNECTION:
  Via Viète's trig solution, the fence bounds correspond
  to the extrema of cos³θ, linking the algebraic and 
  trigonometric derivations.

PDF: fence_to_formula.pdf

title: Bayesian HMC Sweep — Best Configs
preview: nn2_barrel config #75 (single 512, AUC 0.9359) and nn1_endcap config #45 (bottleneck 128-64-128, AUC 0.8610) are top performers.
content:
HYPERPARAMETER SWEEP RESULTS — v5/v6 calibration

BEST CONFIGS:
  nn2_barrel   config #75   single 512            AUC 0.9359  ★
  nn1_endcap   config #45   bottleneck 128-64-128  AUC 0.8610

ARCHITECTURES TESTED:
  cone | bottleneck | single | pyramid
  across nn1/nn2 × barrel/endcap

RRM PENALTY VECTOR:
  v = [1 − AUC,  σ_k/σ_max,  U/U_max]
  where σ_k = k-fold variance, U = HMC posterior spread

POSSIBLE 4D EXTENSION:
  Add ABCD closure quality as 4th penalty dimension.
  Would require running the full ABCD background estimation
  first — currently still TODO.

title: Solar Flare Visualization — Canvas Architecture
preview: HTML5 Canvas solar viz with granular surface, color palettes, prominence eruptions, flare spots, border haze slider, zoom widget.
content:
COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE:
  Layer 0 — Background star field (static, pre-rendered)
  Layer 1 — Solar disc with granular surface texture
  Layer 2 — Chromosphere haze (adjustable intensity)
  Layer 3 — Prominence eruptions (physics-based arcs)
  Layer 4 — Flare spots (UV/X-ray color mapping)
  Layer 5 — Corona (radial decay, color palette driven)

AUDIO REACTIVE MAPPING:
  ΔRMS         → limb brightness pulse
  Low band     → corona expansion radius
  Mid band     → haze opacity
  High band    → flare spot spawn rate
  Transients   → prominence eruption trigger

CONTROLS:
  border haze slider — corona/chromosphere blend
  zoom widget        — canvas scale + pan
  palette selector   — SDO AIA / SOHO EIT / custom

STATUS: Prominence eruption physics needs damping fix.
Corona radial decay tuning in progress.
